title: racosperma baueri subsp. asperum
intro: Racosperma baueri subsp. asperum, also known as Bauera aspera, is a plant species native to eastern Australia. It is used in traditional medicine for its purported medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant has been reported to have health benefits such as wound healing and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating minor cuts and scrapes. Bauera aspera can be prepared into herbal remedies such as teas, tinctures, and ointments, which are used to support overall health and well-being.
